lines.text: "FLYING SAUCER NEWS IN BRIEF"
NEW BOOKS NOW AVAILABLE FROM AFSCA:
Price includes 15¢ mailing charge. California
residents also add 4¢ sales tax.)
COUNCIL OF SEVEN LIGHTS by George Van
Tassel, now reprinted - $3.65.
THE ADVENT OF THE COSMIC VIEWPOINT by
ryant Reeve - $6.15.
FLYING SAUCERS THROUGH THE AGES by Paul
Thomas - $4.15.
OTHER TONGUES, OTHER FLESH by George
unt Williamson, now reprinted - $6.10.
SECRET OF THE ANDES by Brother Philip - $4.
FLYING SAUCERS AND THE THREE MEN by
lbert K. Bender - $4.15.
FLYING SAUCERS - SERIOUS BUSINESS by
rank Edwards - $6.10.
INCIDENT AT EXETER by John G. Fuller -$6.10.
THE HOLLOW EARTH by Dr. Raymond Bernard,
ow available again in soft cover for $3.15.
PAST CONVENTIONS & SAUCER ACTIVITIES:
EBRUARY 5, 6, 1966: Dr. Frank E. Strange's 4th
nnual UFO Space and Science Convention was held at
he Blarney Castle Inn Annex in Los Angeles.
UNE 4, 5, 6, 1966: Dr. Daniel W. Fry's Man in
pace Symposium took place at Dunsmuir, near Mt.
hasta in Northern California.
UNE 25, 26, 27, 1966: Buck Nelson's 10th Annual
pacecraft Convention was held at Buck's Mountain
iew Ranch; Route 1, Box 236; Mountain View, Mo.
BACK ISSUES of UFO INTERNATIONAL are now
elling 11 issues for $3.00, while they last (issue
#1 is now out of print). There is much timeless and
aluable information available in these magazines.
OUT OF PRINT: 1. FLYING SAUCERS: TOP
ECRET by Major Donald E. Keyhoe is now out of
rint, although it is listed in our new brochure.
ome saucer books have occasionally gone out of
rint without notice.)
BACK ISSUES #6 and 11 are now out of print. We
ill have only a few copies left of #9 which we have
iced at $1.00 each in an effort to preserve them for
rious researchers and collectors. Please do not
der these items, or any other materials which we
not advertise.
PRICE CHANGE: The price has gone up on two
the better known books in the saucer field, which
must now order from England: 1. THE SKY
EOPLE by Brinsley le Poer Trench, was $4.65,
is now $5.10, postpaid. 2. ROAD IN THE SKY by
eorge Hunt Williamson, was $4.15. It is now $5.10,
stpaid. California residents please add 4¢ tax.
"UFOI" ISSUE #23 CORRECTIONS: Page 4, line
the date should be April 24, 1964, instead of
65. On page 4, line 1 of the 7th paragraph, insert
ster, via telepathy" after "They told him --."
SEATTLE, WASHINGTON area residents please
te: AFSCA Unit #25 meets every 4th Friday at the
attle Public Library, Room 325 at 7:30 P. M.,
der the capable direction of Mr. Leverett G.
llman. The meeting is free, and all who are
erested in the Flying Saucer subject are invited to
me to listen and to participate in the discussion.
ZIP CODES ARE IMPORTANT: If the address
el on this magazine does not have your zip code
mber on it, please notify us as to what your num-
r is. If you don't know your number, call your
al Post Office and then let us know. Thanks!
AFSCA BROCHURES AND SAUCER-PHOTO
ST CARDS, sent to friends and acquaintances, are
ood way to help spread the word about Flying
Saucers. Brochures are 1¢ each (this just allows
the postage to mail them to you). Saucer postcards
are 50 for $1.00.
WHEN WRITING AFSCA, if you wish a personal
reply to a specific inquiry, please enclose a stamped,
self-addressed envelope. No replies can otherwise
be sent. General information may be obtained from
our brochures and magazines. We are sorry that we
cannot have a lengthy correspondence with all the
hundreds of persons who would like us to do so, but
unfortunately we have neither the time nor the help
for it -- and our mail has been greatly increased in
the last few months.
SAUCER NEWS CLIPPINGS: Your response has
been wonderful! We've received so many clippings
from you helpful people that we simply hadn't the
space to print all your individual names and cities.
So we say a big and sincere THANKS to all of you.
And please do keep 'em coming! (One reminder:
please DON'T write on or near the clippings.)
WEDDING BELLS rang on Saturday, June 11,
for contactee Elary J. Willsie and Sophia Maria
Olivares of Mexico City. The ceremony took place
at Los Angeles, California. Among those present
were Sophia's parents and her younger sister from
Mexico City; Mrs. Maud Willsie, mother of the
groom; and your editor, who was also official photog-
rapher for the occasion. Mr. and Mrs. Willsie will
live in Los Angeles. Congratulations and best wishes,
Elary and Sophia!
MEL NOEL, former Air Force Lieutenant and
fighter pilot, is fast becoming very well known for his
lectures on his amazing experiences with Flying
Saucers while on active duty in the service. He has
been speaking to capacity crowds wherever he goes.
On Thursday, June 23, 1966, the personable Mr.
Noel spoke to a standing-room only crowd at the
Aeronautical Sciences Building on Beverly Blvd.,
West Los Angeles. We hear that he is booked to
speak at 57 colleges across the nation! Recently he
appeared on the Louis Lomax TV Show, and has been
heard on many radio shows. He leaves shortly for
Mexico City, where he has been invited by an
government group to speak. He has been promised
some interesting experiences by a local contactee
there, and we look forward to hearing his report on
his return! Good work, Mel, and good luck!
SID PADRICK, contactee from Watsonville,
Calif., who spent two hours aboard a space craft,
was recently in Los Angeles when he was guest
speaker at the regular monthly meeting of the Ingle-
wood Unit of Understanding on Saturday, June 18,
1966. Meetings are held on the 4th Saturday of the
month at the Inglewood Business and Professional
Woman's Club; 820 Java St., Inglewood, at 8:00 P.M.
Donation is $1.00. Information: Mrs. Robert
Forrester, Program Chairman; P. O. Box 146;
Inglewood, Calif. 90306. Phone: 677-5214.
Mr. and Mrs. Lee Yates. Phone: 293-4743.
NEW AFSCA UNITS:
45. AFSCA Unit #45; Everett, Washington;
Ray and Jean Sebring, Co-Directors;
1032 S. W. 126th St.; Seattle, Wash. 98146.
46. AFSCA Unit #46; Ben Blazs, Director;
3057 Electric; Lincoln Park, Mich. 48146.
Several other new AFSCA Units have been formed
and will be listed in the next issue (#25). Persons
interested in forming AFSCA Units in their cities
should contact AFSCA Headquarters for information.
